All 4 specified files are referenced.

assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 Commit command explicitly includes: "git commit --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' -m ..."

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201' and states 'This creates a branch named TC-9201 from the target branch main.' Line 8 states: 'Target Branch: main (extracted from the Target Branch section of the task description)'. Step 10 Push and PR section: 'gh pr create --base main'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md 'Error handling' section: 'All handlers and service methods return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs... Errors are wrapped using .context() to provide human-readable error messages'. 'Module structure' section: 'All domain modules in modules/fundamental/src/ follow a consistent three-part structure: model/ -- data structs, service/ -- business logic, endpoints/ -- HTTP route handlers'. Both patterns explicitly identified with examples.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 8: 'Target Branch: main (extracted from the Target Branch section of the task description)'. Step 1 line 26: 'Target Branch: main -- this is the branch to use as PR base'. Step 5: 'creates a branch named TC-9201 from the target branch main'. Step 10: 'The PR targets main (the Target Branch extracted from the task description)'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1.5 'Verify Description Integrity': 'Would retrieve issue comments via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201) and search for comments whose body starts with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:. If no digest comment is found, proceed with a warning: "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced." This follows the backward compatibility behavior specified in shared/description-digest-protocol.md: when no digest is found, proceed normally rather than blocking execution.'
